content: HFpEF diagnosis requires: (1) signs/symptoms of HF, (2) LVEF ≥50%, (3) evidence of structural heart disease (LVH, LA enlargement) or diastolic dysfunction (elevated filling pressures). Scoring systems: H2FPEF score and HFA-PEFF algorithm incorporate clinical, echocardiographic, and biomarker data. Natriuretic peptides may be lower than in HFrEF; NT-proBNP >125 pg/mL or BNP >35 pg/mL supports diagnosis. Treatment evidence is limited compared to HFrEF. EMPEROR-Preserved and DELIVER trials showed SGLT2 inhibitors reduce HF hospitalizations in HFpEF (now Class 2a recommendation). Diuretics essential for volume management. Blood pressure control critical (target <130/80). ARNI may provide benefit (PARAGON-HF showed trend toward benefit, especially in women and EF <57%). MRA showed modest benefit in TOPCAT trial (Americas cohort). Treat comorbidities: atrial fibrillation (rate/rhythm control, anticoagulation), obesity (weight loss), sleep apnea (CPAP), diabetes, anemia. Exercise training improves functional capacity. No proven mortality-reducing therapy yet—major evidence gap.
